steps.title: Identify the sentence's role
steps.content: The underlined sentence immediately follows specific examples and announces what those examples illustrate: “This phenomenon...is called reduplication,” followed by a definition of the general concept. This is the signature structure of a definition statement—naming and explaining the principle behind the examples.

steps.title: Match the role to the answer
steps.content: Choice D correctly describes this role: the sentence provides a general definition of the phenomenon discussed. The other three options misidentify what the sentence does—explaining geographic origin (A), contrasting languages (B), or providing translations (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
